parks.name: Maasai Mara National Reserve
parks.description: One of Africa’s greatest safari destinations, renowned for the dramatic Great Migration of millions of wildebeests.
parks.mainDescription: The Maasai Mara National Reserve is one of Africa’s most renowned wildlife conservation areas and forms the northern extension of Tanzania’s Serengeti ecosystem. Covering vast rolling grasslands dotted with acacia trees, the reserve supports one of the highest concentrations of wildlife in the world. It is particularly famous for the annual Great Migration, during which millions of wildebeest, zebras, and gazelles move across the plains in search of fresh grazing.
parks.mainDescription1: This dramatic migration attracts large numbers of predators, including lions, cheetahs, leopards, and crocodiles, making the Maasai Mara one of the best places in Africa to observe predator-prey interactions. In addition to the migration, the reserve is home to elephants, buffalo, giraffes, hippos, and over 450 species of birds.

lodges.name: Alan Walker Serian Camp
lodges.description: Experience the ultimate in luxury and comfort at Alan Walker Serian Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: Tucked into a remote corner of the Maasai Mara ecosystem, Alan Walker Serian Camp is one of the reserve's most intimate and coveted addresses. With just five standard tents and three luxury tents, the camp operates on an exclusive, small-group basis that ensures a deeply personal safari experience rarely found in more commercial properties. The camp sits within a private conservancy bordering the main reserve, granting guests access to vast, uncrowded terrain where the big cats hunt undisturbed and the famous wildebeest migration passes in overwhelming numbers each year.
lodges.mainDescription1: The design of the camp draws from the classic East African safari tradition  weathered canvas, dark timber, lantern light, and earthy textures  yet every detail has been considered with a discerning eye. Meals are served in open-sided dining areas where the sounds of the bush fill the air, and evenings are spent around the fire listening to the distant calls of hyenas and lions. The dedicated team of guides and rangers are among the most knowledgeable in the Mara, offering game drives that go well beyond the familiar circuits to reveal the landscape's quieter, more extraordinary stories.

rooms.name: Standard Tent
rooms.description: Our Standard Tents are intimate and beautifully considered  canvas walls, a hand-carved four-poster bed dressed in white linen, and a private veranda that looks directly onto the Mara plains. Each tent has an ensuite bathroom with a hot shower, solar lantern lighting, and enough space to feel truly immersed in the landscape while remaining entirely comfortable.

rooms.name: Luxury Tent
rooms.description: The Luxury Tents at Alan Walker Serian are the pinnacle of classic East African safari living. Larger in footprint and richer in detail, these tents feature a clawfoot soaking tub positioned to face the bush, a dedicated dressing area, premium handwoven textiles, and a wrap-around veranda furnished with deep canvas chairs. Ideal for couples seeking total immersion in the Mara without compromising on comfort.

includes: Twice-daily game drives in the Maasai Mara
All local beverages (soft drinks, beers, house wines, non-premium spirits)
Guided bush walks with armed ranger
Sundowner drinks on the Mara plains

lodges.name: Spirit of the Maasai Mara
lodges.description: Enjoy breathtaking views and exceptional service at Mara Serena Safari Lodge.
lodges.mainDescription: Set on elevated ground overlooking the open plains of the Maasai Mara, Spirit of the Maasai Mara Lodge combines the grandeur of its surroundings with the ease of a well-appointed full-service lodge. The property's architecture takes inspiration from the land itself  sweeping rooflines echo the horizon, natural stone grounds the structure to the earth, and wide-open spaces draw the eye outward to the grasslands where wildlife moves freely in every direction. It is a lodge built not to compete with the landscape, but to celebrate it.
lodges.mainDescription1: Inside, the rooms are spacious and carefully furnished, each featuring a private terrace designed for nothing more than stillness and observation. The lodge's restaurant serves fresh, locally inspired cuisine paired with thoughtfully selected wines, while the communal lounge and pool area provide a welcoming retreat during the midday heat. Whether guests are tracking a pride of lions at dawn or sipping sundowners as the sky turns amber and violet over the Mara triangle, Spirit of the Maasai Mara delivers an experience that is both refined and genuinely wild.

rooms.name: Standard Room
rooms.description: Standard Rooms at Spirit of the Maasai Mara are spacious and thoughtfully furnished, each opening onto a private terrace with unobstructed views across the rolling savannah. King-size or twin bed configurations are available, and the ensuite bathroom features both a shower and a deep soaking tub. Natural stone finishes, locally crafted furniture, and warm earth tones give each room a strong sense of place.

rooms.name: Deluxe Room
rooms.description: The Deluxe Rooms are our most expansive accommodation, featuring a generous living area, an oversized private terrace with direct savannah views, a minibar stocked daily, and a bathroom with dual vanities and a rainfall shower. Designed for those who want the full measure of the Mara experience  a room to return to after a long day in the field and feel completely at ease.

lodges.name: Entumoto Safari Camp
lodges.description: Experience the perfect blend of comfort and nature at Entumoto Safari Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: Entumoto Safari Camp offers an unpretentious and authentic gateway into the heart of the Maasai Mara. Designed for travellers who want to be close to the action without the weight of five-star formality, the camp blends comfort with a genuine connection to the bush. Solar lighting, canvas tents, and a communal campfire create an atmosphere that feels honest  a place where the focus remains firmly on the wildlife, the land, and the people who have called this landscape home for generations.
lodges.mainDescription1: The camp's location within the greater Mara ecosystem puts guests within striking distance of the most productive game-viewing corridors in Kenya. Morning game drives set out early into open savannah where cheetahs hunt at sunrise and elephants move in quiet herds through the acacia thickets. Entumoto also maintains close ties with the local Maasai community, offering visitors meaningful cultural exchanges that deepen the context of everything they witness in the wild. It is a camp for those who come to the Mara not just to see, but to understand.

rooms.name: Standard Tent
rooms.description: Entumoto's Standard Tents are honest and well-made  roomy canvas units on raised timber platforms with twin or double beds, a private veranda, and an ensuite bathroom with a reliable hot shower. Solar lighting keeps the footprint low and the atmosphere warm, and every tent is positioned to make the most of the morning light and the sounds of the bush at night.

rooms.name: Family Tent
rooms.description: The Family Tents at Entumoto are designed for groups travelling together, with a king bed in the main sleeping area and two additional singles in a connected section. A shared veranda and a generous ensuite bathroom complete the unit, making it practical and comfortable for multi-generational safari parties. Perfect for families with children experiencing the Mara for the first time.

parks.name: Nairobi National Park
parks.description: The world’s only wildlife park set against a capital city skyline, where lions roam just minutes from they city.
parks.mainDescription: Nairobi National Park is one of the most unique wildlife parks in the world, located just 7 kilometers from Nairobi’s city center. It is the only national park adjacent to a capital city, offering visitors an opportunity to experience a real African safari without leaving the urban environment. Spanning 117 square kilometers, the park is home to over 100 mammal species and 400 bird species, making it a perfect destination for wildlife lovers, photographers, and conservation enthusiasts.
parks.mainDescription1: The park is also one of the most important sanctuaries for the endangered black rhino, making it a key conservation area for Kenya’s wildlife protection efforts. Visitors can enjoy classic safari game drives while observing animals roaming freely against the striking backdrop of Nairobi’s skyline. In addition to wildlife viewing, the park features walking trails, picnic sites, and educational centers such as the Nairobi Animal Orphanage and the David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust, which focuses on elephant conservation.

lodges.name: The Emakoko
lodges.description: Choose between our luxury Cliff Suites, River Suites, or our Private House. Make yourself at home.
lodges.mainDescription: The Emakoko is one of Kenya's most celebrated lodges, occupying a dramatic position on the cliffs above the Mbagathi River at the southern boundary of Nairobi National Park. Just forty-five minutes from the international airport, it offers an arrival experience unlike any other  stepping from the chaos of the city into a world of birdsong, leopard territory, and sweeping views across open parkland. The lodge's ten luxury suites are divided between cliff-edge rooms with panoramic vistas and river-level suites nestled in lush riparian forest, each designed with meticulous attention to comfort and natural beauty.
lodges.mainDescription1: Owned and managed with deep personal care, The Emakoko has earned its reputation not only for the quality of its rooms and cuisine, but for the warmth of its team and the intimacy of the experience it provides. Guests can observe rhinos and lions from an outdoor deck before sitting down to an exceptional dinner prepared using fresh, locally sourced ingredients. The combination of outstanding guiding, beautiful surroundings, and effortless hospitality has made The Emakoko a defining first or last stop on any Kenyan safari itinerary  and for many, the highlight of the entire trip.

rooms.name: Luxury Suites
rooms.description: The Emakoko's Luxury Suites are divided between cliff-edge rooms perched above the Mbagathi gorge and river-level suites tucked into the riparian forest below. Each suite features a king-size bed, a private veranda designed for wildlife watching, a fireplace for cool evenings, and a bathroom with both a rain shower and a deep soaking tub. Natural stone, hand-selected art, and locally woven textiles make every suite feel like a considered home rather than a hotel room.

rooms.name: The Private House
rooms.description: The Private House at The Emakoko is an exclusive-use villa accommodating up to four guests in its own self-contained world. With a private lounge, dining terrace, plunge pool, dedicated vehicle and guide, and fully staffed service, it offers the intimacy of a private safari home within one of Kenya's most extraordinary settings. Guests booking the Private House enjoy full access to all lodge facilities while having complete seclusion whenever they choose.

amenities: Private Veranda with Park & River Views
Ensuite Bathroom with Rain Shower & Bathtub
includes: All soft drinks, beers, house wines & non-premium spirits
Morning and evening game drives in Nairobi National Park
Transfers to The Sheldrick Wildlife Trust & Giraffe Centre
Guided walks in Kitengela conservation area
Airport transfers from JKIA & Wilson Airport (min. 2 guests)

lodges.name: Nairobi Tented Camp
lodges.description: Authentic safari atmosphere with spacious canvas tents, en-suite bathrooms, solar lighting, and private verandas.
lodges.mainDescription: Nairobi Tented Camp occupies a quiet, well-wooded section of land bordering Nairobi National Park, offering a genuine tented safari atmosphere within one of the world's most remarkable urban wildlife settings. The camp's spacious canvas tents are elevated on wooden platforms and fitted with en-suite bathrooms, solar lighting, and private verandas that look out over the surrounding bush. It is a property designed for guests who want the texture of a real safari camp  the sounds of the night, the smell of the earth, the sense of exposure  without sacrificing comfort.
lodges.mainDescription1: The camp serves as an ideal base for early-morning game drives into Nairobi National Park, where lions, rhinos, giraffes, and buffalos roam freely against the extraordinary backdrop of the city skyline. The communal areas are open and sociable, centred around a shaded lounge and a fire that draws guests together in the evenings for conversation about the day's sightings. For travellers passing through Nairobi at the start or end of a longer safari, or for those on shorter trips who want to make the most of the city's remarkable ecological neighbours, Nairobi Tented Camp offers an experience that is both convenient and genuinely memorable.

rooms.name: Single Tent
rooms.description: The Single Tents are ideal for solo travellers or couples looking for an authentic tented safari experience within reach of Nairobi. Each tent sits on a raised wooden platform with a double bed, ensuite hot shower, private veranda, solar lighting, and charging points. Simple, well-executed, and deeply atmospheric  particularly in the early mornings when the park's wildlife begins to stir just beyond the canvas.

rooms.name: Double Tent
rooms.description: Our Double Tents offer more generous proportions for couples or families, with a king bed, a separate sitting area, and a larger ensuite bathroom with both a shower and a soaking tub. The private veranda is furnished with reclining chairs and a low table  perfectly positioned for morning coffee as the park wakes up, or sundowners as the Nairobi skyline shifts into its evening colours.

includes: Morning game drive in Nairobi National Park

lodges.name: Ololo Safari Lodge
lodges.description: Originally a Kenyan homestead, Ololo has transformed into a charming boutique lodge that feels both welcoming and exclusive.
lodges.mainDescription: Ololo Safari Lodge is a charming and characterful property set on a private estate bordering the fence line of Nairobi National Park. Originally a Kenyan family homestead, the lodge has been thoughtfully transformed over the years into a boutique retreat that retains all the warmth and personality of a private home while delivering the services and standard of a serious luxury lodge. The gardens are lush and beautifully maintained, the architecture is unpretentious and inviting, and the views across the park from the terrace suites are as remarkable as anything the Kenyan highlands have to offer.
lodges.mainDescription1: The lodge's eight suites each have their own distinct character, blending Swahili antiques, locally crafted textiles, and carefully chosen art in spaces that feel lived-in and intimate rather than styled for a brochure. Afternoon tea on the lawn has become something of an institution at Ololo  a civilised pause in the day that perfectly captures the old Kenya charm the lodge so effortlessly embodies. With game drives into the park departing from the front gate and the sounds of lions and hyenas drifting across the property at night, Ololo offers a rare combination of comfort, culture, and genuine wilderness that is difficult to find anywhere else so close to a capital city.

rooms.name: The Terrace Suite
rooms.description: The Terrace Suites at Ololo are the lodge's signature rooms  each one distinct in character, blending Swahili antiques, handcrafted textiles, and a private garden terrace that opens directly onto the park fence line. A king-size bed, a clawfoot bathtub, a fireplace, and curated bookshelves give the rooms a lived-in warmth that is rare in safari accommodation. Wildlife moves freely on the other side of the fence, and the suites are positioned to make the most of every passing animal.

rooms.name: The Family Wing
rooms.description: The Family Wing at Ololo is a self-contained suite designed for groups or families travelling together. A master bedroom with a king bed connects through a private lounge to two additional rooms, creating a flexible configuration that works for adults and children alike. The shared terrace overlooks the lodge gardens and the park beyond, and the suite has its own entrance  providing both togetherness and privacy in equal measure.

amenities: Ensuite Bathroom with Soaking Tub & Shower
includes: Morning game drive in Nairobi National Park

parks.name: Amboseli National Park
parks.description: A photographer’s paradise where giant elephant herds roam beneath the towering backdrop of Mount Kilimanjaro.
parks.mainDescription: Amboseli National Park is one of Kenya’s most famous wildlife destinations, celebrated for its spectacular scenery and exceptional elephant viewing opportunities. The park lies near the Kenya–Tanzania border and offers stunning views of Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa’s highest mountain. On clear mornings and evenings, the snow-capped peak provides a dramatic backdrop to the park’s open savannah and wetlands.
parks.mainDescription1: Although relatively small in size compared to other Kenyan parks, Amboseli is incredibly rich in wildlife. Large herds of elephants roam the plains and marshlands, while lions, cheetahs, hyenas, giraffes, zebras, and numerous antelope species are commonly seen. The park’s wetlands, fed by underground streams from Mount Kilimanjaro’s melting snow, attract a wide variety of birdlife and other animals throughout the year.

lodges.name: Elerai Camp
lodges.description: Elerai Camp is located in a quiet, unspoilt, unique setting on a 5000-acre private conservation area 12 kms south east of Amboseli.
lodges.mainDescription: Elerai Camp is positioned within a 5,000-acre private conservation area to the south-east of Amboseli National Park, offering an experience of the greater Amboseli ecosystem that feels genuinely unspoilt. The camp sits at the base of the Tanzanian border hills, surrounded by open savannah and acacia woodland that stretch uninterrupted toward the shimmering plains below Kilimanjaro. On clear mornings, the mountain reveals itself in its full scale  snow-capped and impossibly close  creating a tableau that never loses its power no matter how many times it is witnessed.
lodges.mainDescription1: With just ten deluxe tents and five cottage units, Elerai keeps its footprint deliberately small. The emphasis throughout the camp is on stillness, simplicity, and a connection to the landscape that feels earned rather than packaged. Game drives explore not only the main park but the private conservancy land, where vehicle density is minimal and encounters with elephants, big cats, and the plains game of the greater Amboseli area take on an intimacy that larger, busier camps cannot offer. The team at Elerai are passionate naturalists, and their knowledge of the land  its wildlife, its Maasai communities, and its conservation challenges  adds a layer of depth to every day spent here.

rooms.name: Deluxe Tent
rooms.description: Elerai's Deluxe Tents are positioned across the conservancy to maximise views of Kilimanjaro. Each tent is spacious with a king or twin bed, ensuite hot shower, private veranda, and solar lighting. The canvas walls and timber floors keep the atmosphere genuine  this is a camp that lets the landscape speak, with the mountain appearing to guests who wake early and step out into the still morning air.

rooms.name: Cottage Tent
rooms.description: The Cottage Tents at Elerai offer more substantial accommodation for guests wanting extra space. Larger than the Deluxe units, each Cottage Tent has a private lounge area, a wider veranda with dedicated wildlife-watching chairs, an ensuite bathroom with both a shower and a deep tub, and views that on clear mornings frame Kilimanjaro directly from the bedroom window. An ideal choice for honeymoon couples or guests staying three or more nights.

includes: Guided nature walk on the conservancy

lodges.name: Tortilis Camp
lodges.description: Tortilis Camp is named after the flat-topped, umbrella thorn tree, the Acacia Tortilis, and is situated in one of Amboseli’s areas of Acacia
lodges.mainDescription: Named after the iconic flat-topped acacia that defines the Amboseli skyline, Tortilis Camp has long been regarded as one of the finest camps in the greater Kilimanjaro ecosystem. Set within a private conservancy on the edge of the national park, the camp offers a privileged vantage point from which the mountain's changing moods  from cloud-wrapped to crystal clear  play out against an ever-moving tapestry of elephant herds, dust clouds, and open sky. The camp itself is beautiful, built with a light hand and a genuine respect for the environment it inhabits.
lodges.mainDescription1: The tents at Tortilis are spacious and elegantly appointed, each positioned to capture the best possible sightlines across the plains and, on fortunate mornings, directly toward the summit of Kilimanjaro. Private outdoor showers, handcrafted furnishings, and a shaded veranda designed for idle observation give the accommodation a sense of considered luxury that sits comfortably within the wild setting. The camp's commitment to conservation extends beyond its borders  Tortilis has been instrumental in supporting community land programs that protect critical wildlife corridors in the region and provide meaningful livelihoods for the Maasai communities who share this remarkable landscape.

rooms.name: Deluxe Tent
rooms.description: Tortilis Camp's Deluxe Tents are elegant and considered, each positioned under the shade of a tortilis acacia with a private outdoor shower that opens to the sky and the mountain beyond. King-size beds with luxury linen, handcrafted furniture, ceiling fans, and a veranda designed for uninterrupted Kilimanjaro viewing make these tents as much a destination as the park itself.

rooms.name: Cottage Tent
rooms.description: The Cottage Tents at Tortilis are the camp's most private and expansive accommodation, set slightly apart from the main cluster. Each Cottage offers a generous bedroom, a separate lounge with a fireplace, a large private veranda, and an ensuite bathroom with a rainfall shower and an outdoor soaking tub framed by the Amboseli plains. A dedicated morning tea service completes the experience for those who want the full measure of a Tortilis stay.

amenities: Private Ensuite Bathroom with Outdoor Shower

lodges.name: Kilima Safari Lodge
lodges.description: Kilima safari camp is located in Amboseli National Park, next to the Kimana Gate Park entrance. It is set out on a 420 acres of a land.
lodges.mainDescription: Kilima Safari Lodge occupies a generous 420-acre site adjacent to Amboseli National Park's Kimana Gate, giving it some of the most accessible and productive game-viewing terrain in the greater Kilimanjaro ecosystem. The lodge takes its name from the Swahili word for mountain, a reference to the breathtaking presence of Kilimanjaro that looms across the park boundary and dominates the horizon from almost every point on the property. Wide-open views, carefully positioned rooms, and an architecture that leans into the landscape rather than imposing on it make Kilima one of Amboseli's most visually striking addresses.
lodges.mainDescription1: The accommodation is generous and well-appointed, with classic and superior tent categories offering private decks, ensuite bathrooms, and furnishings that blend East African safari heritage with contemporary comfort. The lodge's elevated central area  incorporating a restaurant, bar, and pool terrace  functions as a living room for guests, a place to gather before and after drives, to compare notes on the day's sightings, and to watch the mountain change colour as the sun drops behind it in the evening. With elephants regularly passing through the property itself and a committed team of guides who know the park's resident animal families by name, Kilima offers a safari experience that is both personal and deeply connected to the land.

rooms.name: Classic Tent
rooms.description: The Classic Tents at Kilima deliver exactly what the name promises  the timeless East African safari experience, well executed and reliably comfortable. King or twin beds, ensuite bathrooms with hot showers, ceiling fans, and private decks all feature as standard, and every tent is positioned across the 420-acre grounds to give guests a meaningful view of Kilimanjaro when conditions allow.

rooms.name: Superior Tent
rooms.description: Kilima's Superior Tents are the lodge's premium accommodation, offering substantially more space, a private lounge area, and a terrace with direct sightlines toward Kilimanjaro's summit. The ensuite bathroom features both a rain shower and a freestanding bathtub, and the décor is elevated throughout with locally crafted art, premium linen, and furniture built by Amboseli artisans. Air conditioning and a stocked minibar complete the picture.

parks.name: Tsavo East National Park
parks.description: Kenya’s largest wilderness, famous for its vast red-earth plains and the iconic red-dusted elephants of Tsavo.
parks.mainDescription: Tsavo East National Park is one of the largest protected areas in Kenya and forms part of the vast Tsavo Conservation Area. Covering more than 13,000 square kilometers, the park is characterized by its expansive semi-arid plains, scattered baobab trees, volcanic hills, and seasonal rivers. The landscape is rugged and dramatic, offering visitors a sense of true wilderness rarely found elsewhere.
parks.mainDescription1: The park is especially famous for its red elephants, which often appear coated in the region’s distinctive reddish soil after dust bathing. Tsavo East supports an impressive variety of wildlife including lions, leopards, cheetahs, giraffes, buffalo, zebras, and numerous bird species. One of the park’s most striking features is the Galana River, which flows through the park and forms the beautiful Lugard Falls, a series of rapids flowing through sculpted rock formations.

lodges.name: Satao Camp
lodges.description: satao camp is an unfenced tented camp in the heart of tsavo east national park in Kenya, it offers a unique and authentic safari experience.
lodges.mainDescription: Satao Camp sits unfenced in the heart of Tsavo East National Park, positioned above a natural waterhole that draws a constant and extraordinary procession of wildlife throughout the day and night. The camp is named after Satao, one of Kenya's most celebrated and beloved tuskers  a great elephant whose memory lives on in the spirit of the place and in the conservation values that underpin everything the camp does. To sit on the viewing deck and watch a herd of Tsavo's iconic red elephants move through the dust toward water as the afternoon light fades is to understand why this vast, rugged wilderness has captured imaginations for over a century.
lodges.mainDescription1: The tents at Satao are well-proportioned and comfortably furnished, each with a private veranda overlooking the bush and the waterhole beyond. The camp operates in a way that prioritises the wildlife and the landscape above all else  there is no fence between the tents and the animals, no manicured lawns or unnecessary infrastructure, just canvas, timber, and the sounds of wild Africa in every direction. The guiding team combines deep local knowledge with genuine enthusiasm, leading drives that range across Tsavo's immense network of game tracks to find the leopards, lions, and vast elephant herds that make this one of Kenya's most compelling safari destinations.

rooms.name: Standard Tent
rooms.description: Satao's Standard Tents are unfenced, unfiltered, and entirely honest  canvas walls, timber floors, twin or double beds with mosquito netting, and a private veranda that sits just metres above the waterhole. Designed for those who come to Tsavo for the wildlife rather than the room, offering solid comfort and genuine immersion in equal measure. Sunsets from the veranda, with elephants moving below in the amber dust, are among the most memorable Tsavo has to offer.

rooms.name: Suite Tent
rooms.description: The Suite Tents at Satao are the camp's most generous offering, featuring a larger bedroom, a private sitting area, and an expanded veranda positioned directly above the waterhole. A clawfoot bathtub, premium linens, and a dedicated morning tray service add to the experience, making these tents the natural choice for guests celebrating a special occasion or those who simply want the best seat in the camp.

excludes: Night game drives (available at extra cost)

lodges.name: Lions Bluff Lodge
lodges.description: Lions Bluff Lodge is a luxury lodge located in Tsavo East National Park, Kenya. It offers stunning views of the park and its wildlife, making it a popular choice for safari-goers.
lodges.mainDescription: Lions Bluff Lodge stands apart in Tsavo East for the sheer drama of its position  elevated high above the Tsavo plains on a rocky bluff, the lodge commands views that extend for miles across the red-earthed wilderness below. From every suite and every shared space, the landscape rolls away in waves of ochre, olive, and deep green, bisected by the dark ribbon of the Galana River and framed by distant volcanic hills. It is the kind of vista that makes the act of simply sitting still feel like an event in itself, and the lodge has been designed around that experience with total conviction.
lodges.mainDescription1: The rooms at Lions Bluff are among the most generously proportioned in the Tsavo region, each featuring a private deck that cantilevers over the escarpment and a bathroom with views across the plains. The interiors draw on the earthy palette of the landscape  raw stone, hand-trowelled plaster, deep ochre linens, and locally crafted furniture that gives the rooms a sense of place as strong as the views beyond the glass. Game drives descend from the lodge into the park below, where red elephants, lion prides, and the occasional leopard reward early departures, while guided walks to the spectacular Lugard Falls on the Galana River offer a more intimate engagement with the extraordinary geology and ecology of Tsavo East.

rooms.name: Standard Room
rooms.description: Lions Bluff Standard Rooms are positioned along the escarpment with private decks cantilevered over the bluff and views that stretch across the Tsavo plains to the Galana River below. King or twin beds, an ensuite bathroom with a rain shower and soaking tub, and a palette drawn from the landscape's ochre and olive tones give the rooms a strong sense of place. Floor-to-ceiling glass on the bush-facing wall ensures the view is present from every point in the room  including from the bath.

rooms.name: Deluxe Room
rooms.description: The Deluxe Rooms at Lions Bluff are the lodge's most elevated accommodation. Each features a private plunge pool on the escarpment deck, a separate lounge and dressing room, a bathroom with dual rainfall showers and a freestanding tub with panoramic bush views, and butler service throughout the stay. The scale of the landscape from these rooms  the plains, the river, the volcanic hills  is simply without comparison in the Tsavo region.

amenities: Ensuite Bathroom with Rain Shower & Bathtub
Elevated Private Deck with Tsavo Plains Views

lodges.name: Kilaguni Serena Safari Lodge
lodges.description: Kilaguni Serena Safari Lodge is a luxury lodge located in Tsavo East National Park, Kenya. It offers stunning views of the park and its wildlife, making it a popular choice for safari-goers.
lodges.mainDescription: Kilaguni Serena Safari Lodge holds a distinctive place in the story of Kenyan conservation  opened in 1962, it was the first lodge ever built inside a national park in Kenya, and it has never lost the sense of occasion that comes with that pioneering history. Set against the backdrop of the Chyulu Hills within the greater Tsavo ecosystem, the lodge overlooks a floodlit waterhole that draws elephants, buffalo, and plains game throughout the day and late into the evening. On clear days, the distant snowfields of Kilimanjaro are visible on the horizon, adding yet another layer of grandeur to a location that scarcely needs it.
lodges.mainDescription1: The lodge has been carefully updated and expanded by Serena Hotels over the decades, maintaining its character and sense of place while adding the services and standards that modern travellers expect. Thirty rooms and a selection of deluxe options are arranged around the central lodge area, each offering views across the waterhole and the bush beyond. The restaurant serves well-executed cuisine informed by local produce, the spa offers a welcome retreat after long days in the field, and the guiding team  among the most experienced in the Tsavo region  leads drives that regularly turn up the lions, leopards, and vast red elephant herds for which this extraordinary wilderness is justly famous.

rooms.name: Standard Room
rooms.description: Kilaguni's Standard Rooms are well-proportioned and carefully finished, each with a private balcony overlooking the floodlit waterhole and, on clear days, the distant snowfields of Kilimanjaro on the horizon. Air conditioning, an ensuite bathroom with both a tub and shower, complimentary Wi-Fi, and the full range of Serena Hotels amenities make these rooms a reliable and comfortable base for exploring the wider Tsavo ecosystem.

rooms.name: Deluxe Room
rooms.description: Kilaguni's Deluxe Rooms offer more generous floor space, a sitting area, a larger private balcony, a premium bathroom with rainfall shower and soaking tub, and enhanced minibar and butler services. The views from the Deluxe balcony  across the waterhole, through the Chyulu Hills, and on exceptional days to the faint profile of Kilimanjaro  are among the most layered and beautiful available anywhere in the greater Tsavo ecosystem.

amenities: Private Balcony with Waterhole & Kilimanjaro Views

parks.name: Lake Nakuru National Park
parks.description: A shimmering alkaline lake sanctuary famous for flamingos and both the black and white rhinos.
parks.mainDescription: Lake Nakuru National Park is a beautiful wildlife sanctuary centered around Lake Nakuru, a shallow alkaline lake located in Kenya’s Rift Valley. The park is internationally famous for the large flocks of flamingos that historically gathered along the lake’s shores, creating a stunning pink spectacle visible from surrounding viewpoints.
parks.mainDescription1: Beyond the flamingos, the park has become one of Kenya’s most important rhino conservation areas and now protects both black and white rhino populations. The park’s diverse habitats include open grasslands, acacia woodlands, rocky escarpments, and waterfalls, supporting wildlife such as lions, leopards, buffalo, giraffes, and numerous bird species.

lodges.name: Sarova Lion Hill Lodge
lodges.description: Experience the ultimate in luxury and comfort at Alan Walker Serian Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: Perched on a wooded hillside inside Lake Nakuru National Park, Sarova Lion Hill Lodge offers an elevated perspective over one of Kenya's most visually spectacular wildlife sanctuaries. The lodge sits within the park boundary, meaning game  including the park's celebrated rhino population and a resident pride of lions  moves freely through the surrounding forest and can regularly be seen from the rooms and common areas without ever boarding a vehicle. It is a genuinely immersive setting, and the lodge has been built to make the most of it at every turn.
lodges.mainDescription1: The rooms are arranged across the hillside in clusters that minimise their visual impact on the landscape while maximising the views available from each private balcony. Inside, the décor is warm and unfussy  locally crafted furniture, earthy fabrics, and a colour palette drawn from the lake, the flamingos, and the acacia woodlands that surround the property. The lodge's central dining and pool area is a lively and welcoming space, particularly at dawn when the lake below turns pink with flamingos and the first game drive vehicles roll out into the cool morning air. For its combination of location, comfort, and value within a national park, Sarova Lion Hill remains one of the Nakuru region's most dependable and satisfying choices.

rooms.name: Standard Room
rooms.description: Sarova Lion Hill's Standard Rooms are set within the wooded hillside, each offering a private balcony with views down through the yellow fever acacias toward the lake below. King or twin beds, air conditioning, an ensuite bathroom, and warm safari-toned interiors make for a comfortable and characterful stay. The forested setting means guests regularly see birds and small mammals from their balconies without leaving the room.

rooms.name: Deluxe Room
rooms.description: The Deluxe Rooms at Sarova Lion Hill are positioned for the best lake views the property has to offer  elevated on the upper hillside with wide private balconies framing Lake Nakuru and, in good flamingo seasons, the pink haze that extends along the shoreline. Larger than the Standard category and finished with upgraded linens, a soaking tub, and complimentary minibar, these rooms are the natural upgrade for guests who want to make the most of the lodge's extraordinary setting.

lodges.name: Lake Nakuru Lodge
lodges.description: Enjoy breathtaking views and exceptional service at Mara Serena Safari Lodge.
lodges.mainDescription: Lake Nakuru Lodge occupies one of the most coveted positions in the national park  a spacious, elevated site within the forest zone that provides both seclusion and sweeping views toward the lake below. The property has developed over many years into a well-established full-service lodge that serves the widest possible range of travellers without ever losing the sense of place that makes Nakuru so compelling. Towering yellow fever acacias shade the pathways between rooms and public areas, and the forest around the lodge is alive with bird activity throughout the day, including the Fischer's turaco, the silvery-cheeked hornbill, and dozens of other species that reward guests who simply pause and look upward.
lodges.mainDescription1: The rooms at Lake Nakuru Lodge offer generous proportions and private terraces that capture the forest light at different times of day. The lodge's spa and wellness facilities provide a genuine counterpoint to the vigour of game driving  a place to decompress, reflect, and absorb the slower rhythms of the landscape. Game drives lead directly into the park from the lodge gates, with rhino tracking among the most sought-after experiences the reserve offers. Whether guests are watching the lake's flamingos turn it pink at dawn, following a black rhino through the dense euphorbia forest at midday, or enjoying sundowners on the terrace as the acacia canopy catches the last of the evening light, Lake Nakuru Lodge provides a setting and a standard of comfort that make every moment feel well spent.

rooms.name: Standard Room
rooms.description: Lake Nakuru Lodge's Standard Rooms are generous and well-appointed, set within the forest zone with private terraces that capture the shifting light filtering through the acacia canopy. Each room features a king or twin bed, an ensuite bathroom with a rain shower and soaking tub, air conditioning, and thoughtfully chosen décor that draws from the colours of the lake and the woodland. The forest setting is alive with birdsong throughout the day.

rooms.name: Deluxe Room
rooms.description: The Deluxe Rooms at Lake Nakuru Lodge offer a significant step up in space and positioning, with larger terraces oriented toward the lake's open water and enhanced bathroom facilities including dual vanities, a freestanding bathtub, and a rainfall shower. A minibar, fireplace, premium toiletries, and upgraded linens complete the picture. The Deluxe category is particularly popular with photographers who appreciate the elevated vantage point for early-morning flamingo light.

amenities: Ensuite Bathroom with Rain Shower & Soaking Tub
Private Terrace with Lake & Flamingo Views

lodges.name: Flamingo Hill Camp
lodges.description: Experience the perfect blend of comfort and nature at Entumoto Safari Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: Flamingo Hill Camp takes its name from the breathtaking flamingo spectacle for which Lake Nakuru is globally renowned, and the camp's position  close to the lakeshore within the national park  places guests at the centre of that spectacle with minimal fuss and maximum honesty. The camp is intentionally small and unpretentious, built from canvas and natural materials with a light footprint on the land and a strong emphasis on the outdoor experience. Early mornings bring the lake closest  its surface reflecting the pink haze of flamingos moving in vast flocks  and the camp's communal areas are oriented to make the most of those unforgettable hours before the sun climbs high.
lodges.mainDescription1: The tents at Flamingo Hill are comfortable and practical, offering the essentials of a good safari camp  hot showers, solid beds, mosquito netting, and a private veranda from which to listen to the lake's soundtrack at night. Meals are taken communally under a shaded banda, creating the sociable atmosphere that is one of the enduring pleasures of tented camp life. Game drives from the camp cover the full circuit of the park, from the rhino sanctuary in the north to the waterfall picnic site in the south, while birdwatching walks along the lakeshore offer an entirely different and deeply rewarding way to engage with this remarkable place.

rooms.name: Standard Tent
rooms.description: Flamingo Hill's Standard Tents are comfortable and unpretentious  spacious canvas units with twin or double beds, hot showers, and a private veranda positioned to face the lake. Solar lighting, eco-friendly toiletries, and thoughtful storage keep the footprint light. The camp's proximity to the lakeshore means that guests can hear the flamingos from their beds on still evenings, which is perhaps the finest amenity any room in Nakuru can offer.

rooms.name: Family Tent
rooms.description: The Family Tents at Flamingo Hill are configured for groups of up to four, with a king bed in the main sleeping area and two additional singles in an adjacent section. The shared veranda is generously sized, the ensuite bathroom has a reliable hot shower, and the canvas structure is well-maintained and weatherproof. A practical, well-priced option for families who want a genuine lakeside tented camp experience.

includes: Flamingo & bird walk along the lakeshore

parks.name: Samburu National Reserve
parks.description: A rugged northern reserve known for rare wildlife species like the Grevy’s zebra, reticulated giraffe, and the gerenuk.
parks.mainDescription: Samburu National Reserve is located in Kenya’s northern frontier along the banks of the Ewaso Nyiro River, which provides a vital water source for wildlife in this otherwise arid landscape. The reserve is known for its rugged beauty, characterized by dry savannah, rocky hills, and acacia woodlands that create a striking contrast with the river’s lush vegetation.
parks.mainDescription1: Samburu is particularly famous for hosting several rare animal species collectively known as the “Samburu Special Five.” These include the Grevy’s zebra, reticulated giraffe, Beisa oryx, Somali ostrich, and the graceful gerenuk, an antelope known for standing on its hind legs to reach leaves. Other wildlife found in the reserve includes elephants, lions, leopards, cheetahs, and crocodiles along the riverbanks.

lodges.name: Samburu Sopa Lodge
lodges.description: Experience the ultimate in luxury and comfort at Alan Walker Serian Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: Samburu Sopa Lodge sits on the banks of the Ewaso Nyiro River in one of northern Kenya's most dramatic and biologically distinctive landscapes. The river is the lifeblood of Samburu  a ribbon of green cutting through an otherwise arid terrain of red rock and sparse bush  and the lodge's position along its southern bank gives guests front-row access to the remarkable concentration of wildlife that gathers here throughout the year. Elephants wade across the shallows, crocodiles bask on the sandy banks, and the Samburu Special Five  Grevy's zebra, reticulated giraffe, Beisa oryx, Somali ostrich, and gerenuk  are regularly seen from the lodge grounds themselves.
lodges.mainDescription1: The lodge is the largest property in the reserve and operates with the full complement of services expected at a Sopa property  a restaurant serving generous and well-prepared meals, a swimming pool that offers cooling relief in the midday heat, and a team of experienced guides who know the reserve's game circuits and resident animal families in intimate detail. Rooms are arranged along the riverbank, each with a balcony designed for unhurried observation of whatever the Ewaso Nyiro delivers on any given day. It is a lodge that combines reliable comfort with a setting of genuine northern frontier wildness, making it one of the most complete options available in the Samburu region.

rooms.name: Standard Room
rooms.description: Samburu Sopa's Standard Rooms are well-proportioned and comfortable, each with a private balcony overlooking the Ewaso Nyiro River and the dry acacia bush stretching toward the reserve's rocky hills. King or twin beds, air conditioning, an ensuite bathroom with shower and tub, and a warm safari palette throughout the room make this a reliable and enjoyable base for days spent exploring northern Kenya's extraordinary wildlife.

rooms.name: Luxury room
rooms.description: The Luxury Rooms at Samburu Sopa are the lodge's signature accommodation, offering a larger floor plan, a wider river-facing terrace, a clawfoot bathtub with open views, premium linens, and butler service throughout the stay. The elevated position above the Ewaso Nyiro means elephants, crocodiles, and the Samburu Special Five are regularly visible from the terrace without ever boarding a vehicle  an extraordinary privilege that makes these rooms worth every shilling.

amenities: Private Balcony with Ewaso Nyiro River Views
includes: Guided cultural visit to Samburu village

lodges.name: Elephant Bedroom Camp
lodges.description: Enjoy breathtaking views and exceptional service at Mara Serena Safari Lodge.
lodges.mainDescription: Elephant Bedroom Camp is one of Samburu's most distinguished and characterful properties, built directly on the bank of the Ewaso Nyiro River at a point where elephants cross daily in family groups of extraordinary size. The camp takes its name from this daily ritual  a procession of giants moving through the water just metres from the tents  and everything about the design, the guiding, and the daily rhythm of the camp is built around making that experience as intimate and unhurried as possible. With eleven tents strung along the riverbank beneath a canopy of fig and acacia, Elephant Bedroom is small enough to feel personal without ever feeling sparse.
lodges.mainDescription1: The tents themselves are beautifully appointed  spacious interiors with outdoor hot showers, handcrafted furniture, and private decks positioned to face the river and the dramatic escarpment rising beyond its northern bank. The guiding team at Elephant Bedroom is among the best in Samburu, equally skilled at tracking the Samburu Special Five through the reserve's open bush terrain and identifying the extraordinary variety of birds  including the Vulturine guineafowl, Eastern yellow-billed hornbill, and Somali bee-eater  that make this part of Kenya a destination of real significance for wildlife photographers and naturalists alike. Evenings at the camp end around a fire on the riverbank with the sounds of the bush and the Ewaso Nyiro moving quietly in the dark.

rooms.name: Standard Tent
rooms.description: Elephant Bedroom's Standard Tents are set directly on the bank of the Ewaso Nyiro River, each with an outdoor hot shower open to the sky, a king-size bed under a canopy of mosquito netting, and a private veranda facing the water and the elephant crossing that gives the camp its name. Lantern lighting, handcrafted furniture, and the constant soundtrack of the river make these tents among the most atmospheric in Kenya's northern reserves.

rooms.name: Luxury Tent
rooms.description: The Luxury Tents at Elephant Bedroom are the camp's most expansive and thoughtfully finished accommodation. A larger canvas footprint encompasses a private lounge area, a dressing room, a bathroom with a copper soaking tub and outdoor rain shower, and a wide veranda with front-row river views. The tent is positioned at the quietest end of the camp, ensuring maximum privacy while remaining close to the communal fire and the camp's excellent dining area.

amenities: Ensuite Bathroom with Outdoor Hot Shower
King-size Bed with Mosquito Net & Luxury Linens
includes: Guided nature walk along the Ewaso Nyiro

lodges.name: Saruni Basecamp
lodges.description: Experience the perfect blend of comfort and nature at Entumoto Safari Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: Saruni Basecamp offers the most accessible entry point into the Samburu experience within the Saruni portfolio  a compact, well-run camp designed for travellers who want to engage deeply with northern Kenya's extraordinary landscape without the full weight of a luxury lodge tariff. The camp's three main tents and five family units are honest and comfortable, fitted with the essentials of a good safari stay and oriented toward the bush rather than inward on themselves. Meals are taken together in a shaded banda, guiding is attentive and informed, and the atmosphere throughout the camp is relaxed and sociable.
lodges.mainDescription1: The camp sits within access of the Samburu National Reserve's core game-viewing areas, giving guests direct exposure to the species that make this part of Kenya unique  most notably the Samburu Special Five and the rich birdlife of the Ewaso Nyiro corridor. Family tents make Saruni Basecamp a particularly practical option for groups travelling with children, and the camp's community partnerships with local Samburu pastoralists add a layer of cultural richness to the stay that is genuinely informative. For those new to northern Kenya or looking for an honest, well-priced introduction to a landscape unlike anything further south, Saruni Basecamp delivers exactly what it promises.

rooms.name: Standard Tent
rooms.description: Saruni Basecamp's Standard Tents are solid, comfortable, and well-suited to the camp's honest safari ethos. Each tent sleeps up to six guests across a combination of bunk and single beds  making them a popular choice for families or friend groups travelling together. An ensuite bathroom with a hot shower, a shaded veranda, and solar lighting complete the setup. The focus is firmly on the landscape and wildlife beyond the tent.

rooms.name: Family Tent
rooms.description: The Family Tents at Saruni Basecamp are configured for groups of four, with a double bed in the primary sleeping area and two singles in an adjacent zone  well suited to parents travelling with children. A private veranda with unobstructed bush views, a dedicated ensuite bathroom, and proximity to the communal lounge and dining banda make these tents a practical and comfortable choice for families new to Samburu's extraordinary northern landscape.

parks.name: Hell's Gate National Park
parks.description: One of the few parks in Africa where visitors can cycle or walk among dramatic cliffs, gorges, and geothermal landscapes.
parks.mainDescription: Hell’s Gate National Park is one of Kenya’s most distinctive parks, known for its dramatic scenery and unique geological features. Located in the Great Rift Valley near Lake Naivasha, the park is named after a narrow break in the cliffs that was once a tributary of a prehistoric lake. Towering red cliffs, deep gorges, geothermal steam vents, and volcanic rock formations create a landscape unlike any other park in Kenya.
parks.mainDescription1: One of the most unusual aspects of Hell’s Gate is that visitors are allowed to explore certain areas on foot or by bicycle, providing a more active and immersive experience than traditional safari parks. Wildlife such as zebras, buffalo, antelopes, and various bird species roam the open plains, while towering rock formations like Fischer’s Tower attract climbers and photographers.

lodges.name: Naivasha Kongoni Lodge
lodges.description: Experience the ultimate in luxury and comfort at Alan Walker Serian Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: Naivasha Kongoni Lodge is a refined lakeside retreat set on the shores of Lake Naivasha, within easy reach of Hell's Gate National Park and the surrounding Rift Valley landscape. The lodge takes its name from the Kongoni  the Coke's hartebeest  one of the more characterful antelope species that graze the open grasslands around the property. It is a place of quiet beauty, where the lake shimmers through papyrus reeds, fish eagles call from the fever trees, and the dramatic silhouette of the Rift Valley escarpment frames every view to the south and west. Guests arrive and almost immediately slow down, drawn into the unhurried pace that Lake Naivasha seems to demand of all who spend time on its banks.
lodges.mainDescription1: The lodge's twenty-two deluxe rooms and ten executive rooms are generously sized and well-appointed, each with a private terrace positioned to capture the lake light across different times of day. Bicycle hire is available directly from the lodge for expeditions into Hell's Gate  one of the few Kenyan parks where cycling among the wildlife is permitted  and the guiding team organises gorge walks, boat rides on the lake, and evening drives through the park that round out the activity programme beautifully. It is a lodge that rewards guests who choose to explore the full breadth of what the Naivasha region offers, from the volcanic drama of Hell's Gate to the hippo pods and birdlife that crowd the lake's shallow margins.

rooms.name: Deluxe Room
rooms.description: Kongoni Lodge's Deluxe Rooms are positioned across the lakeshore grounds with private terraces that open toward the shimmering surface of Lake Naivasha and the papyrus-lined banks beyond. King-size beds, rain showers, locally crafted furnishings, and a palette drawn from the Rift Valley's earth tones make these rooms a genuinely restorative retreat after a day of cycling through Hell's Gate or hiking the gorge.

rooms.name: Executive Room
rooms.description: The Executive Rooms at Kongoni are the lodge's premium offering  larger, more elevated, and fitted with a private terrace that commands an expansive view of Lake Naivasha from above the treeline. A clawfoot bathtub positioned to face the lake, a minibar, premium linens, and enhanced butler service make these rooms a meaningful step up. Ideal for couples on a special occasion or guests who want the full lodge experience after an active day in the field.

amenities: Ensuite Bathroom with Rain Shower & Bathtub
Private Terrace with Lake Naivasha Views
includes: Bicycle hire for Hell's Gate National Park

lodges.name: Elsamere Lodge Naivasha
lodges.description: Enjoy breathtaking views and exceptional service at Mara Serena Safari Lodge.
lodges.mainDescription: Elsamere Lodge carries one of the most storied names in Kenyan conservation history. The original homestead of Joy and George Adamson  authors of Born Free  the property sits in a mature garden on the southern shore of Lake Naivasha, shaded by ancient yellow fever acacias and inhabited by black-and-white colobus monkeys who treat the grounds as their own. The lodge has been carefully preserved and expanded since Joy Adamson's death, retaining the intimate, garden-party atmosphere of the original house while adding enough rooms and facilities to welcome a wider range of guests who come as much for the history and setting as for a base from which to explore Hell's Gate.
lodges.mainDescription1: Afternoon tea on the lawn at Elsamere has become something of a pilgrimage for visitors to the Naivasha region  a ritual of scones, Earl Grey, and colobus monkeys overhead that captures the particular quality of old Kenyan hospitality the lodge embodies so naturally. The rooms are comfortable and unpretentious, with a style that feels consistent with the property's character rather than imposed upon it. The Adamson conservation documentary plays each evening for guests interested in the history of the place and the remarkable story of Elsa the lioness. For those who want a meaningful, story-rich, and genuinely beautiful base in the Naivasha area, Elsamere offers something no amount of design budget can replicate  real history, lived in and lovingly kept.

rooms.name: Standard Room
rooms.description: Elsamere's Standard Rooms are comfortable and full of character  each one reflecting the lodge's history as the home of Joy and George Adamson, with personal touches, vintage furniture, and a style that feels genuinely inherited rather than designed. Lake or garden views, a ceiling fan, ensuite bathroom with shower, and complimentary Wi-Fi feature throughout. The grounds are inhabited by black-and-white colobus monkeys and over 200 bird species.

rooms.name: Superior Room
rooms.description: The Superior Rooms at Elsamere are the lodge's most spacious category, each offering a direct lake view from a private veranda and upgraded bathroom facilities with a rainfall shower and soaking tub. The interiors continue the lodge's distinctive aesthetic  Adamson-era photographs, African art, natural wood floors, and the unhurried atmosphere the original house embodied. A perfect room from which to watch the sun set over the lake as the colobus troops settle into the fever trees above.

amenities: Historic Afternoon Tea on the Lawn

lodges.name: The Great Rift Valley Lodge
lodges.description: Experience the perfect blend of comfort and nature at Entumoto Safari Camp.
lodges.mainDescription: The Great Rift Valley Lodge commands one of the most spectacular positions available to any lodge in Kenya  perched at the very edge of the Rift Valley escarpment above Lake Naivasha, the property looks out over a drop so vast and so visually arresting that the first view from the terrace tends to stop guests completely in their tracks. The lodge and its villa suites are arranged along the escarpment rim, each one positioned to capture the full sweep of the valley floor stretching away below  the lake's silver surface, the volcanic hills of Longonot and Suswa in the middle distance, and on clear days the endless roll of the Rift toward the south.
lodges.mainDescription1: The lodge serves as a genuinely all-encompassing destination in its own right. In addition to game drives and activities in Hell's Gate National Park below, the property features a golf course, a spa, extensive grounds for walking, and direct access to the escarpment's extraordinary viewpoints. The villa accommodations offer the most expansive experience  large, self-contained units with fireplaces, private terraces, and the kind of space that encourages extended stays. Whether guests come for the views, the activities, the golf, or simply to sit at the edge of one of the great geological wonders of the world and let the scale of it settle in, The Great Rift Valley Lodge provides a setting and standard of service equal to the extraordinary landscape it inhabits.

rooms.name: Lodge Room
rooms.description: The Lodge Rooms at The Great Rift Valley Lodge are perched at the escarpment's edge with views across Lake Naivasha and the valley floor that rank among the finest from any hotel in Kenya. King or twin beds, air conditioning, an ensuite bathroom with a shower and tub, and a private balcony designed for nothing but looking out at the scale of the Rift Valley make these rooms an experience in their own right. Access to the golf course, spa, and swimming pool is included.

rooms.name: Longonot villas
rooms.description: The Longonot Villas are The Great Rift Valley Lodge's most exclusive accommodation  fully self-contained units set right at the lip of the escarpment with private plunge pools, open-plan living areas, fireplaces for cool highland evenings, and panoramic terraces looking directly over the valley and across to Mount Longonot. A private butler, dedicated vehicle, and full access to the lodge's facilities make the villas the natural choice for guests who want the Rift Valley entirely to themselves.

amenities: Private Balcony with Rift Valley Views
includes: Use of swimming pool & golf course
Morning guided walk along the Rift escarpment

parks.name: Kisite Mpunguti Marine Park
parks.description: A vibrant underwater paradise along Kenya’s coast celebrated for its coral reefs, playful dolphins, and crystal-clear snorkeling waters.
parks.mainDescription: Kisite-Mpunguti Marine Park is a protected marine ecosystem located along Kenya’s southern coastline near the historic town of Shimoni. The park encompasses a group of small islands, coral reefs, and crystal-clear waters that support one of the richest marine biodiversity areas in the Western Indian Ocean. The coral reefs provide habitat for hundreds of species of fish, sea turtles, and other marine creatures, making the park a paradise for snorkeling and scuba diving enthusiasts. Visitors frequently encounter playful dolphins that inhabit the waters surrounding the park, adding to the unforgettable marine experience.
parks.mainDescription1: Although relatively small in size compared to other Kenyan parks, Amboseli is incredibly rich in wildlife. Large herds of elephants roam the plains and marshlands, while lions, cheetahs, hyenas, giraffes, zebras, and numerous antelope species are commonly seen. The park’s wetlands, fed by underground streams from Mount Kilimanjaro’s melting snow, attract a wide variety of birdlife and other animals throughout the year.
